task: We're a B2B SaaS company ($2M ARR, 15 paying customers, $130K average ACV). We have an inbound SDR team of 2 and 3 AEs. Marketing generates leads but sales says the leads are low quality. Marketing says sales doesn't follow up. We have HubSpot. Design a lead lifecycle and handoff process to fix this.
expect: Identifies the classic marketing-sales misalignment problem and names it: no agreed MQL definition
Proposes specific stage definitions for this company's size and ACV (Lead → MQL → SAL → SQL → Opportunity)
Defines clear MQL criteria (not vague — specific score or behaviour trigger) that both teams can agree on
Includes an SLA for SDR follow-up (4 business hours for standard MQLs, 5 minutes for demo requests)
Proposes a handoff record format so sales receives enough context to have a meaningful first conversation
Addresses the accountability gap: who owns what stage and what the consequence of SLA breach is

task: Build a lead scoring model for a DevOps tooling company (CI/CD platform). ICP: engineering teams at 50–500 person tech companies. Champion: DevOps engineers or platform engineers. Economic buyer: VP Engineering or CTO. Key signals that predict purchase: trial signup, pricing page visit, documentation visits (suggests active evaluation), GitHub integration page view.
expect: Produces both a fit scoring table (company size 50-500: full points; company size range with tiered scores; title DevOps/Platform Engineer: full champion points; VP Eng/CTO: economic buyer points) and engagement scoring table
Trial signup and pricing page visit should be among the highest-point actions (20+ points each)
Documentation visits and GitHub integration page should be medium-high scoring (8–15 points) given the technical evaluation signal they represent
Proposes a MQL threshold with rationale
Addresses negative scoring (personal email domains, student titles, competitor's company domain)

task: Our RevOps metrics show: MQL→SQL conversion rate is 55% (healthy). SQL→Opportunity rate is 45% (below benchmark of 70-85%). Opportunity→Close rate is 28% (at benchmark). Average sales cycle is 67 days. ACV is $18K. We have 8 AEs and 4 SDRs. Where is the revenue leak and what should we fix?
expect: Correctly identifies SQL→Opportunity as the primary leak (45% vs. 70-85% benchmark — significant gap)

Diagnoses likely root causes: SDRs are booking discovery calls but AEs aren't creating opportunities (qualification not happening), or discovery calls are revealing disqualifiers that aren't being caught at SQL stage
Recommends tightening the SQL definition (ensure BANT/MEDDPICC basics are confirmed before SQL is created)
Calculates the revenue impact: improving SQL→Opp from 45% to 65% with the given pipeline volume
Does NOT over-rotate on the opportunity→close rate (28%) since it's within benchmark — focuses the recommendation on the actual bottleneck
